metadata.scheme_name: Amit Gautam Memorial Scholarship (B.Tech in Electronics Engineering – 4th Year)

raw_data.details: The “Amit Gautam Memorial Scholarship (B. Tech in Electronics Engineering - 4th Year)” scheme is implemented by Govind Ballabh Pant University of Agriculture and Technology, Pantnagar, under the Department of Agriculture, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to support meritorious students pursuing a B. Tech in Electronics Engineering who are in their 4th year of study by providing a one-year scholarship.
raw_data.benefits: The eligible student will receive ₹7,000 per year as a scholarship.
raw_data.eligibility: The applicant must be a student. The applicant must be enrolled in the 4th year of the B. Tech (Electronics Engineering) course at Govind Ballabh Pant University of Agriculture and Technology, Pantnagar. The scholarship is awarded for a duration of one year only.
raw_data.application_process: OfflineFirst, applications are invited from students in the prescribed format. Thereafter, a committee constituted by the Vice-Chancellor evaluates the received applications as per the prescribed procedure. Based on the committee’s recommendations, the Vice-Chancellor grants approval, following which the selected students receive the scholarship amount.
raw_data.documents_required: Documents are required in accordance with the university guidelines prescribed for this scheme.
raw_data.scheme_name: Amit Gautam Memorial Scholarship (B.Tech in Electronics Engineering – 4th Year)

faqs.question: Who is eligible for this scholarship?
faqs.answer: Students who are enrolled in the 4th year of the B. Tech (Electronics Engineering) program at Govind Ballabh Pant University of Agriculture and Technology, Pantnagar, are eligible to apply.

faqs.question: What is the amount of the scholarship?
faqs.answer: The scholarship provides financial assistance of ₹7,000 for one year.

faqs.question: For how long is the scholarship awarded?
faqs.answer: The scholarship is awarded for a duration of one year only.

faqs.question: How can a student apply for the scholarship?
faqs.answer: Applications are invited from students in the prescribed format provided by the University.

faqs.question: Who evaluates the applications?
faqs.answer: A committee constituted by the Vice-Chancellor evaluates the applications according to a prescribed procedure.

faqs.question: How is the final decision made?
faqs.answer: The committee submits recommendations to the Vice-Chancellor, who grants final approval for the scholarship.

faqs.question: When do students receive the scholarship amount?
faqs.answer: Students receive the scholarship amount after the Vice-Chancellor grants approval based on the committee’s recommendations.
